# Implementation Plan: ML Architecture Design

**Branch**: `054-ml-architecture-design` | **Date**: 2026-03-14 | **Spec**: GitHub Issue #54
**Input**: GitHub Issue #54 + `/docs/ARCHITECTURE_SHIFT_TO_LOCAL_ML.md`

**Note**: This plan was generated by the `/speckit.plan` command.

## Summary

Define the comprehensive architecture for transitioning TrashMail Panda from external LLM-based email classification (OpenAI GPT-4o-mini) to a locally-trained ML model using ML.NET. This is a **documentation-only deliverable** producing three architecture documents: ML_ARCHITECTURE.md (system architecture), FEATURE_ENGINEERING.md (feature extraction pipeline), and MODEL_TRAINING_PIPELINE.md (training workflow). The design must preserve the existing provider-agnostic architecture and Result pattern while introducing ML-specific components.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: .NET 9.0 / C# 12+  
**Primary Dependencies**: ML.NET (planned), existing provider framework (`IProvider<TConfig>`, `BaseProvider<TConfig>`), Microsoft.Extensions.DI/Logging  
**Storage**: SQLite + SQLCipher (existing encrypted storage), extended for email feature vectors and full email archive  
**Testing**: xUnit + Moq + coverlet (existing)  
**Target Platform**: Cross-platform desktop (macOS, Windows, Linux) — console-first TUI  
**Project Type**: Desktop application (transitioning from Avalonia UI to console TUI)  
**Performance Goals**: Classification <100ms per email batch (local inference), model training <5min for incremental updates  
**Constraints**: 100% local processing (privacy-first), configurable storage cap (default 50GB), offline-capable  
**Scale/Scope**: Single-user personal email classifier, 10K–100K emails in training corpus

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

| Principle | Status | Notes |
|-----------|--------|-------|
| I. Provider-Agnostic Architecture | ✅ PASS | ML classifier will implement a new `IClassificationProvider` following `IProvider<TConfig>` pattern, replacing `ILLMProvider` for classification |
| II. Result Pattern | ✅ PASS | All new ML service methods will return `Result<T>` — no exceptions thrown from ML components |
| III. Security First | ✅ PASS | Email data stored locally with SQLCipher encryption; no external API calls for classification; feature vectors stored encrypted at rest |
| IV. MVVM with CommunityToolkit.Mvvm | ✅ N/A | This is a documentation deliverable; no UI changes. Future console TUI will use different patterns |
| V. One Public Type Per File | ✅ PASS | Architecture design will specify one public type per file for all new components |
| VI. Strict Null Safety | ✅ PASS | All new types will have explicit nullability annotations |
| VII. Test Coverage & Quality Gates | ✅ PASS | Architecture will specify testing requirements: 95% for ML providers, 100% for data pipeline security |

**GATE RESULT**: ✅ ALL PASS — proceed to Phase 0

## Project Structure

### Documentation (this feature)

```text
specs/054-ml-architecture-design/
├── plan.md              # This file
├── research.md          # Phase 0: ML.NET research, feature engineering patterns
├── data-model.md        # Phase 1: Email feature schema, model metadata
├── quickstart.md        # Phase 1: Quick reference for implementers
├── contracts/           # Phase 1: IClassificationProvider, IFeatureExtractor interfaces
└── tasks.md             # Phase 2 output (NOT created by /speckit.plan)
```

### Source Code (repository root)

```text
docs/
├── ML_ARCHITECTURE.md           # Deliverable 1: System architecture spec
├── FEATURE_ENGINEERING.md       # Deliverable 2: Feature extraction spec
└── MODEL_TRAINING_PIPELINE.md   # Deliverable 3: Training workflow spec
```

**Structure Decision**: This feature produces only documentation artifacts in `/docs/`. No source code changes. The architecture documents will define the component structure for future implementation issues (#55+).
